evidence_effect: prospective B-R contrast identity and restoration design only; no response, comparator, numeric registration, conformance result, calibration, execution, control outcome, or scientific result

q013_contrast_registry.question: Does repeated temporally distributed formation differ from one formation pulse with the same total deposited quantity?
matched: total native formation transfer into the registered carrier
baseline route opportunity and later probe class
varied: number and temporal distribution of attributable formation events
q013_contrast_registry.interpretation_boundary: distinguishes temporal formation structure from accumulated formation quantity; it cannot establish active-history dependence unless the complete causal continuation state is also matched

q013_contrast_registry.question: Does B-R organization loss differ from generic route-mass loss of the same amount?

candidate_relation.operation: native export from m_e to isolated d_e under the DEC-028 lifecycle

control_relation.operation: remove q/2 from s_e and q/2 from m_e into an explicit isolated control destination

matched: registered branch timing at the comparison boundary
preconditions: both s_e and m_e satisfy the registered q/2 availability bound
candidate and control destinations remain isolated from the route and encounter
no removed coherence is destroyed or hidden
q013_contrast_registry.interpretation_boundary: distinguishes organization contrast from generic mass loss; the outcome is not predetermined

q013_contrast_registry.question: Do different attributable formation histories produce different futures when complete causal continuation state and future inputs are equal?
matched: future native schedule and registered observation horizon
varied: audit lineage and exact execution identity where required to retain that history
q013_contrast_registry.formed_arm_claim_rule: only an arm with valid costly attributable formation may satisfy the formation requirement

interpretation.matching_futures: supports current-composite-state sufficiency at the tested boundary
interpretation.difference_after_verified_complete_match: reopens active-history or missing-state interpretation without automatically supporting B-R
interpretation.match_not_constructible: realization or restoration limitation, not negative L03 evidence

q013_separation_rules: the three contrast identities are non-substitutable
formation-quantity matching and export-mass matching may not share one generic quantity_matched result identity
quantity matching does not imply complete-state matching
complete-state matching does not imply equal exact execution bytes when audit histories differ
relocation, false-trace, clamp, withdrawal, and hidden-router controls retain separate identities
I03 owns contrast meaning and stable IDs; I04 owns response and comparator binding; I06 owns exact values and construction

exact_composite_execution_identity.purpose: exact branch reconstruction, save/load/reset, audit, and replay
required_components: native topology, node coherence, conductance, packet ledger, pending events, event time, phases, scheduler, and budgets
lifecycle cursor, eligible and consumed receipts, pending reservation, scheduled export, and settlement state
source, carrier, reservoir, continuation-node, edge, and route-role bindings
fixed encounter opportunity, parent checkpoint, request schedule, and branch identity
audit lineage and every other serialized field
exact_composite_execution_identity.equality_rule: exact canonical component identities and one manifest identity match before continuation

causal_continuation_equivalence_projecti.purpose: state-matched history discrimination and equal-input continuation comparison
causal_continuation_equivalence_projecti.included: every field capable of changing registered future lifecycle, native evolution, request construction, admission or refusal, distance policy, or observation
causal_continuation_equivalence_projecti.excluded: only fields prospectively validated as audit-only and unread by native runtime and all producers
causal_continuation_equivalence_projecti.equality_rule: projection equality is necessary but must be followed by bounded equal-input continuation; a digest match alone is not continuation evidence
causal_continuation_equivalence_projecti.cross_history_rule: different-history arms restore exactly to themselves and may differ in exact execution identity while their causal continuation projections match

q015_operations.save: retain all native and RCAE components plus their component digests and one composite manifest
q015_operations.load: return a fully validated composite branch or fail closed before scientific continuation; partial, stale, one-sided, mismatched, or cross-bound component loads are invalid
q015_operations.reset: restore the declared native and RCAE baselines together; implicit rebase and one-sided or repeated-reset drift are invalid
q015_operations.fork: create each scientific branch from one validated clean parent checkpoint with a fresh immutable branch identity
q015_operations.invasive_probe: an admitted Q-007 probe terminates its branch; delayed or repeated probes fork from clean unprobed parents
q015_operations.field_limited_refusal: compare the post-attempt native identity with the pre-step identity that already contains the scheduled request and require exact atomic neutrality
q015_operations.replay: restore the same clean parent, apply the same registered inputs, and reproduce disposition plus registered state projections

required_comparisons: each state-matched history arm restored exactly to itself
paired state-matched history arms under equal causal projection and identical future inputs
atomic refusal replay from the same clean parent
registered_outputs_include: route-local response projection later bound by I04

fail_closed_conditions: native state paired with the wrong policy, route, opportunity, intervention, or reset baseline
stale policy readout or pending-work mismatch
unregistered field excluded from causal projection without an audit-only proof
continuation begins after partial load or failed composite validation
equal-input continuation diverges outside the later registered field-specific equality rule
deferred_to_i06: save load reset fork and replay commands
exact reset values and branch counts
reopening_conditions: a contrast cannot be constructed without conflating its matching relation with another contrast
a continuation-relevant component is absent from both identity layers
audit-only lineage must be read to implement B-R
a complete composite cannot be restored through a fail-closed manifest-coordinated interface
bounded conformance finds unequal continuation after a verified restore or reset
the native refusal boundary cannot be reconstructed atomically
new graph evidence changes the admitted restoration or B-R provider contract
